title: cota tinctoria subsp. virescens
intro: Cota tinctoria subsp. virescens, also known as the green dyer's chamomile or greenfield knapweed, is a subspecies of the dyer's chamomile plant, native to Europe and Western Asia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities, which make it useful for treating wounds, skin conditions, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Cota tinctoria subsp. virescens include its ability to soothe and calm irritated tissues, reduce inflammation, and promote a healthy gut microbiome. In herbal preparations, Cota tinctoria subsp. virescens can be used as a tincture, tea, or infused oil to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
